message:
feat(providers): add OpenRouter routing overrides, stream error handling, and dynamic provider names

Three provider-layer improvements for the OpenAI-compatible backend:

1. OpenRouter Routing Overrides:
   - Add build_request_json() that serializes the request, then applies
     routing config overlays from compat.openRouterRouting
   - apply_openrouter_routing_overrides() merges top-level keys (models,
     provider, route) into the request body only when the provider is
     "openrouter" and routing config is present
   - This enables OpenRouter's fallback/priority routing without
     requiring upstream request struct changes

2. Stream Chunk Error Handling:
   - Add OpenAIChunkError struct to deserialize error objects embedded in
     SSE stream chunks (used by OpenRouter and some other providers)
   - Map finish_reason "error" to StopReason::Error alongside the
     existing "content_filter" mapping
   - Propagate error.message from chunks into partial.error_message so
     callers can surface upstream provider failures (e.g. timeouts)

3. Dynamic Provider Names in Errors:
   - Replace hardcoded "openai" in error messages with &self.provider so
     authentication failures and HTTP errors correctly identify the
     actual provider (groq, cerebras, openrouter, etc.)

Includes unit tests for error-reason mapping, routing override
application, and existing stream behavior regression coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
